title: CASE expressions with constants are not evaluated correctly
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_case (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value INT CHECK (value >= 0));
INSERT INTO test_case (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10), (2, 'test2', -5), (3, 'test3', 20);
SELECT CASE WHEN value < 0 THEN name ELSE 'valid' END AS status FROM test_case;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INT, description VARCHAR(100));
key_bug_features: CASE expressions with constants not evaluated correctly
